description: The Douste-Blazy reform aimed to curb the deficit of the French social security system by introducing patient parcours (pathways) and raising taxes. The plan faced massive resistance from unions and the medical community, leading to months of debate and protests.

key_events.description: Government unveils the reform plan to the public.
Large-scale protests and strikes by medical staff.
Parliamentary debate begins amid social tension.
Final bill passed into law despite low public approval.

calibration_notes: Exemplifies how unpopular austerity-driven health reforms are pushed through by executive power, showing divergence between legislation and public sentiment.
